name: Potential Malicious Application Shimming
notes:
This rule triggers when application shimming activity by monitoring registry modifications is detected. Attackers can use retrocompatibility functionalities to perform privilege escalation, install backdoors, etc.

Custom Databases can be found in the following locations:
  %WINDIR%\AppPatch\Custom
  %WINDIR%\AppPatch\CustomSDB
  %WINDIR%\AppPatch\AppPatch64\Custom

Note: Tune the Reference Set Shims Allowlist with any custom Shim.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful Registry Modification
when the event matches Rule Name (custom) contains any of T1138
when the event matches Registry Value Data (custom) is not N/A
when the event matches LOWER("Registry Value Data") MATCHES '.*?\\(apppatch\\(?:apppatch64\\)?custom(?:sdb)?).*' AQL filter query
when any of Registry Value Data (custom) are contained in any of Shims Allowlist - AlphaNumeric
TD_list: APPLY Potential Malicious Application Shimming on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful Registry Modification
AND when the event matches Rule Name (custom) contains any of T1138
AND when the event matches Registry Value Data (custom) is not N/A
AND when the event matches LOWER("Registry Value Data") MATCHES '.*?\\(apppatch\\(?:apppatch64\\)?custom(?:sdb)?).*' AQL filter query
AND NOT when any of Registry Value Data (custom) are contained in any of Shims Allowlist - AlphaNumeric
RED_list: Event description: Detects application shimming activity by monitoring registry modifications.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
